nameMeanings.name: Daughter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Dawtrey, see Daughtry and also Daughters . The surname is most common in Yorkshire and Sussex, where the medieval Dawtrey family held lands in the 12th century.

variants.name: Daughters
meanings: English (London): variant of Dafter, with post-medieval excrescent -s, apparently from Middle English doughter, dohtor, douter (Old English dohtor) ‘daughter’, often pronounced in early modern English as dafter. For the name to be borne by a man, such that it became a hereditary surname, is surprising, though there are occasional instances of men being named in this way (unless they are clerical errors or it was a surname acquired by the retainers of an heiress of an important family). Alternatively, alternatively Daughter, Daughters might be a simplified pronunciation of Dawtry (see Daughtery ), a surname well evidenced in Yorkshire and Sussex, where Doughter and Daughter also occur, in which case the change to Dafter will have arisen through misinterpretation.

variants.name: Daughtry
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Dawtrey, a habitational name with fused French preposition d(e), for someone from Hauterive in Orne, France, named from Old French haute rive ‘high bank’ (rendered into Latin Alta Ripa). The medieval Dawtrey family had lands in Yorkshire, Lincolnshire, and Sussex in the 12th century.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: Daughter of Ralph FitzHubert was born in 1108, in England, United Kingdom as the daughter of Ralph Fitzhurbert and ?. She married Gilbert de Meinell about 1125. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter. She died in her hometown.
